saints.name: Saint Leo the Great, Pope of Rome

troparion.text: O Champion of Orthodoxy, and teacher of holiness, / the enlightenment of the universe and the inspired glory of true believers. / O most wise Father Leo, your teachings are as music of the Holy Spirit for us! / Pray that Christ our God may save our souls!

kontakion.text: Seated upon the throne of the priesthood, glorious Leo, / you shut the mouths of the spiritual lions. / With divinely inspired teachings of the honored Trinity, / you shed the light of the knowledge of God upon your flock. / Therefore, you are glorified as a divine initiate of the grace of God.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Cosmas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Saint Agapitos the Confessor and Wonderworker, Bishop of Synnada in Phrygia

troparion.text: Champion of Orthodoxy, teacher of purity and of true worship, / the enlightener of the universe and the adornment of hierarchs: / all-wise father Agapitos, your teachings have gleamed with light upon all things. / Intercede before Christ God to save our souls.

saints.name: Saint Flavian the Confessor, Patriarch of Constantinople

troparion.text: Champion of Orthodoxy, teacher of purity and of true worship, / the enlightener of the universe and the adornment of hierarchs: / all-wise father Flavian, your teachings have gleamed with light upon all things. / Intercede before Christ God to save our souls.

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Nicholas, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Colman, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.
